title: temporal property language + Dwyer prelude + scopes
manager: A temporal property language where users pick a pattern and fill the holes — response, precedence, absence, universality, existence, with scopes — instead of writing raw LTL and getting it subtly wrong. The Dwyer catalog covers about 92% of real-world specifications, so most properties become fill-in-the-blank; raw temporal logic stays as an escape hatch for the rest. This is what makes formal specification usable by people who aren't logicians.
cs: `property p = pattern(args) [scope];` grammar (Dwyer prelude); lowering safety->reachability product, liveness->Büchi + lasso search; `lint --suggest-properties` structural mapper; counterexample = tape; fsl#1360.
